items.front: 9-1-1: Three triggers for special handling of flight inspection ops
items.back: Special participation of ground personnel, specific communications, or radar operation capabilities. Coordinated with facilities before departure.

items.front: Special participation of ground personnel, specific communications, or radar operation capabilities trigger special handling (9-1-1).
items.back: Three criteria that make a flight inspection operation require special handling, per 9-1-1 Note 2.

items.front: 9-1-1: Why strict schedule adherence for flight inspection?
items.back: Automatic recording equipment requires uninterrupted flight; limited fleet means schedule must be kept.

items.front: 9-1-2: What happens if a 'recorded run' is interrupted?
items.back: The entire procedure must be reinitiated. Necessitates full runway length, protection of critical areas, and full procedure up to and including missed approach.

items.front: 9-1-2: FLIGHT VAL vs Flight Check — priority difference
items.back: Flight Check (FAA) = special handling as required. FLIGHT VAL (non-FAA) = no additional priority granted, despite similar activities.

items.front: 9-1-2: What three requirements must be protected during a recorded run?
items.back: Full length of runway, protection of any critical areas, entire procedure up to and including the missed approach as needed.

items.front: 9-1-2: When can you ask a flight inspection pilot to deviate from planned action?
items.back: Only to preclude an emergency situation. Otherwise clear according to pilot request as soon as practicable.

items.front: 9-1-3: Route of flight orbital range for preplanned automatic flight check
items.back: 6 NM to a maximum of 40 NM from the facility, depending on type of inspection.

items.front: 9-1-3: Altitude range for preplanned automatic flight check
items.back: From 1,000 feet above the antenna site up to the minimum en route altitude (MEA).

items.front: 6 NM to 40 NM; 1,000 feet above antenna to MEA.
items.back: Route orbital range and altitude range for preplanned automatic flight checks per 9-1-3.

items.front: 9-1-3: What must be flown during a commissioning flight check?
items.back: All SIDs, STARs, airways, DME fixes, and approaches must be flown.

items.front: 9-1-3: Four prohibitions for flight check aircraft handling
items.back: Do NOT: change route, change altitude, impose ATC delays (except emergencies), or change previously assigned discrete beacon code of special radar accuracy aircraft.

items.front: 9-1-3: When can close-to-surface flight inspection maneuvers be performed?
items.back: Only during daylight visual meteorological conditions.
